channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok’s Canada unit wants court review of shut-down order
item.description: TikTok’s Canadian unit said it has filed an emergency motion with Canada’s Federal Court seeking a judicial review of an order that the company shut down its operations in the country on national security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Federal judge slaps down Automattic, granting temporary injunction to WP Engine in ongoing WordPress squabble
item.description: The battle between WordPress owner Automattic and WP Engine seemingly struck US federal Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin as rather one-sided, as she ruled against Automattic on Tuesday and granted WP Engine the preliminary injunction it sought.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple's latest iPhones get the gift of more AI as holiday shopping season heats up
item.description: Apple is pumping more artificial intelligence into the latest iPhones during the holiday shopping season.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Amazon Autos Launches in 48 US Cities, Revolutionizing Car Buying Experience
item.description: Amazon's new online platform allows customers to browse, purchase, and trade-in vehicles, making car buying more transparent and convenient.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Pentagon announces new AI office as it looks to deploy autonomous weapons
item.description: The Defense Department on Wednesday announced a new office focused on accelerating and adopting artificial intelligence (AI) technology for the military as it aims to deploy autonomous weapons in the near future.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Police departments are embracing AI-powered video surveillance. This company is leading the effort
item.description: A red Hyundai SUV makes its way through downtown San Francisco on a bright August afternoon. The car looks innocuous, but a day earlier, police had linked it to the break-in of another car and added it to a so-called hot list of wanted vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Ainsley Harris, Fast Company

item.title: Dashboards, sharing threat intelligence helping USDA improve cybersecurity responses
item.description: When it comes to cybersecurity, the more data you have, the easier it is to identify anomalies and stop adversaries. The post Dashboards, sharing threat intelligence helping USDA improve cybersecurity responses first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Supreme Court allows investors' class action to proceed against microchip company Nvidia
item.description: The Supreme Court is allowing a class-action lawsuit that accuses Nvidia of misleading investors about its past dependence on selling computer chips for the mining of volatile cryptocurrency to proceed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: TikTok files challenge against Canadian government order to dissolve its business in the country
item.description: TikTok has challenged a Canadian government order to shut down the Chinese video-sharing app’s business operations in the country that was imposed over national security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Android 2025 preview: 7 things to watch
item.description: As we look ahead to a whole new year of green-tinted Googley goodness, I’m feeling both guardedly excited — and curiously like Bill Murray in the movie Groundhog Day. Google’s got some pretty weighty pivots in the works for the Android arena right now.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Finding purpose at the intersection of tech and transport
item.description: Transport for New South Wales was first established in 2011, and since then, the culture of putting customers and communities at the center of everything, and partnering with operational agencies, private operators, and industry to deliver passenger focus services and projects, has been a constant.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: New US CIO appointments, December 2024
item.description: Movers & Shakers is where you can keep up with new CIO appointments and gain valuable insight into the job market and CIO hiring trends.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: GM is pulling the funding for its Cruise robotaxi company
item.description: General Motors is pulling funding from robotaxi company Cruise in order to focus on its own autonomous and assisted-driving systems.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: About the Best Places to Work in IT
item.description: The 2025 honorees have been announced! See our Best Places to Work in IT 2025 special report for the complete list of honorees, major trends from this year’s survey, and much more.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IC’s new OSINT standards cover open source data, AI services
item.description: As OSINT becomes a major focus for the intelligence community, the new standards give analysts and collectors explicit instructions for citing certain data. The post IC’s new OSINT standards cover open source data, AI services first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Atmospheric river and potential bomb cyclone will bring messy, dangerous weather to East Coast
item.description: The East Coast is due for a whiplash-inducing rainy, windy and potentially dangerous stretch of weather.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Johnson pours cold water on KOSA push
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) cautioned Republicans against rushing to pass a bill to protect kids and teens online on Tuesday as the GOP prepares to take control of the White House and Congress.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Databricks unveils synthetic data generation API to help evaluate agents faster
item.description: As enterprise demand for building multi-agent systems continues to grow, data infrastructure services providing firm, Databricks, is updating its Mosaic AI Agent Evaluation module with a new synthetic data generation API that will help enterprises evaluate agents faster.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Chinese online retailer Temu suspended in Vietnam
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Vietnam has suspended the operations of Chinese online retailer Temu after it failed to meet a government deadline to register the company by the end of November.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KRON4
